title: Argentinian Garlic Beef Empanadas
description: A classic South American pastry filled with seasoned ground beef and spices.
instructions: 2. In a large bowl, combine ground beef, brown sugar, crumbled garlic, and argentinian seasoning blend. Mix well with your hands until just combined. Be careful not to overmix.
3. In a separate bowl, combine flour and salt. Gradually add the flour mixture to the beef mixture and mix until a dough forms.
4. Turn the dough out onto a floured surface and knead for about 5 minutes, until smooth and elastic.
5. Divide the dough into 8-10 equal pieces. Roll each piece into a ball and flatten slightly into a disk shape.

6. Place a tablespoon of the beef mixture in the center of each disk. Brush the edges with a little water and fold the dough over to form a half-moon shape. Press the edges together with a fork to seal.
7. Brush the tops of the empanadas with egg and cut a few slits in the top to allow steam to escape.
8. Bake the empanadas for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
9. Serve warm with your favorite dipping sauce.
